div.p: Ghost.1 Zacharias was a priest of God, and officiating in the Temple,2 and John was a priest after his father, and held the keys of the Aaronic Priesthood,3 and was called of God to preach the Gospel of the kingdom of God.4 The Jews, as a nation, having departed from the law of God5 and the Gospel of the Lord,6 prepared the way for transferring it to the Gentiles.7

But, says one, the kingdom of God could not be set up in the days of John, for John said the kingdom was at hand.8 But I would ask if it could be any nearer to them than to be in the hands of John. The people need not wait for the days of Pentecost9 to find the kingdom of God, for John had it with him, and he came forth from the wilderness crying out, “Repent ye, for the kingdom of heaven is nigh at hand,”10 as much as to say, “Out here I have got the kingdom of God, and you can get it, and I am coming after you; and if you don’t receive it, you will be damned;”11 and the scriptures represent that all Jerusalem went out into John’s baptism.12 There was a legal administrator, and those that were baptized were subjects for a king;13 and also the laws and oracles of God were there; therefore the kingdom of God was there; for no man could have better authority to administer than John; and our Savior submitted to that authority Himself, by being baptized by John;14 therefore the kingdom of God was set up on the earth, even in the days of John.15

There is a difference between the kingdom of God and the fruits and blessings that flow from the kingdom;16 because there were more miracles, gifts, visions, healings, tongues, &c., in the days of Jesus Christ and His apostles, and on the day of Pentecost, than under John’s administration, it does not prove by any means that John had not the kingdom of God, any more than it would that a woman had not a milkpan because she had not a pan of milk, for while the pan might be compared to the kingdom, the milk might be compared to the blessings of the kingdom.

John was a priest after the order of Aaron,17 and had the keys of that priesthood,18 and came forth preaching repentance and baptism for the remission of sins,19 but at the same time cries out, “There cometh one mightier than I after me, the latchet of whose shoes I am not worthy to stoop down and
